layman_headline: Crypto markets are gripped by fear — patience is the smartest move right now.
layman_explanation: The crypto market is in 'Extreme Fear' mode at 22/100, driven by geopolitical tensions in the Strait of Hormuz, a looming U.S. inflation report, and $425 million flowing out of Bitcoin ETFs. Bitcoin is holding around $62,600 but could easily slip below $60,000. In this environment, most altcoins are bleeding heavily and none of the candidates offer a compelling risk-reward setup with clear technical confirmation.
layman_action: Stay in cash and wait. The fear index is extreme, which historically precedes good buying opportunities — but the catalysts driving fear (Iran conflict, CPI data, government Bitcoin sales) haven't resolved yet. Watch for stabilization before committing capital.
expert_reasoning: Every candidate has significant headwinds. MON (RSI 36.85, bullish marubozu) is the closest to a setup but trades below all MAs with R:R of only 0.79x — insufficient for a swing entry. KITE shows a bullish engulfing but just crashed 14.9% in 24h and is in a steep downtrend. BONK has a -1.4 chart bias with bearish marubozu — a falling knife. JTO has the highest chart bias (+0.40) but RSI is already at 59 after an 18.5% weekly drop, suggesting the bounce may be exhausted. The macro backdrop is hostile: $425M ETF outflows, U.S. government selling $297M in seized crypto to Coinbase Prime, and the Iran-Hormuz conflict threatening risk assets broadly. Fear & Greed at 22 is a reliable contrarian indicator historically (80% win rate in our data), but the unresolved geopolitical and macro catalysts mean the fear may deepen before bottoming.
risk_factors: Iran-Hormuz conflict escalation could trigger a broader risk-off selloff pushing BTC below $60K
CPI report within hours could spike rate hike expectations further, pressuring all crypto
U.S. government selling $297M in seized BTC/ETH to Coinbase Prime adds direct sell pressure
watch_list: BTC price action around the $60,000 support level — a break below invalidates alt setups
U.S. CPI report release and market reaction in the next 8 hours
Iran-Hormuz situation developments and impact on global risk sentiment
market_context: Bitcoin is clinging to $62,600 amid extreme fear (22/100), geopolitical tensions, $425M ETF outflows, and government crypto liquidations. BTC dominance at 56.1% signals capital rotation away from alts — a hostile environment for altcoin swing trades.
context_banner: Multiple unresolved risk events demand patience — extreme fear creates opportunity but only after catalysts resolve.

key_signals.description: Extreme Fear at 22/100 with improving trend — historically a reliable contrarian buy zone (80% win rate), but unresolved catalysts suggest fear may deepen first.
U.S. government moved $288M in seized BTC/ETH to Coinbase Prime, creating direct sell pressure on the market.
US spot Bitcoin ETFs posted $425M outflow after a brief rebound, signaling institutional risk-off positioning.
Bitcoin has less than 8 hours to hold $60K before Hormuz conflict escalation takes over — binary risk event imminent.
Best candidate MON has RSI at 36.85 approaching oversold, but below all moving averages with inadequate risk/reward ratio of 0.79x.
